parameters: Journalist
"Mr. Steven Steel, about the 'Steel Ball Run' race that
begins in two days. You have explained things before, but
for the readers around the world, let us move on."
Steel
"The goal will be New York! Making it mankind's first
American cross-country horseback race!"
Steel
"The champion will receive Fifty Million American Dollars,
which will be deposited into the individual's East and West
bank accounts!"
Steel
"As for the runner-ups, 2nd place: receive $1 million,
Steel
"And so on, continuing through 10th place."
Steel
"Please read the rulebook for more details."
Steel
"As long as the participant is age 16 or older, nationality,
sex, race, and pro or amateur status wil not be questioned.
There is a necessary participation fee, which is $1,200 per
person."
Journalist
"Tell us about the sponsors of this contest."
Steel
"First of all, your news company, East & West Tribunal, has
exclusive coverage of this race. Others include the
respective publishing companies, hotel groups including the
beach hotel, B&C Packaging Corporation..."
Steel
"...Winchester Firearm Production, Horizontal Continental
Railroad Corporation, Speedwagon Oil Corporation, and many
others across America."
Journalist
"Mr. Steel, I believe Baron Rocatugo of German nobility has
announced participation in machine called called an
'automobile'?"
Steel
"Ah, yes, I approved of that. The race's identity is the
pioneering spirit! When the railroads were first opened
in North America, people said this:
Steel
'The age of the horse is over.'\FS[28]
Steel
This race accepts that challenge. Come with any kind of
ground transporation, machine or horse!"
Steel
"This race is a first in history! Nobody will be able to
predict anything that happens! With one machine, or with one
horse, nobody has ever raced without rest, with competition
for over 50 miles a day, for a total of 3,500 miles!"
Journalist
"Is that estimation a limit to how far the horses can run?
That means it would take roughly 60 to 80 days to reach the
goal."
Steel
"Well, there was a record of quarterhorses running 1800
miles between Missouri and California in 12 days, but that
was a relay of 400 horses."
Steel
"And during the Crimean War, an Arabian horse ran 90 miles in
a day, but the rider died of exhaustion."
Steel
"In this race, in spite of the environmental conditions of
the wastelands and uncharted territories, the exchange of
horses is prohibited and will result in disqualification."
Journalist
"There are some complaints about the fee. It's roughly the
same as a worker's monthly salary..."
Steel
"I don't see the issue. We will cover their travel expenses,
medical expenses, and food expenses in the case of dropping
out."
Journalist
"Will participants be allowed to carry arms during the race?
If so, what if they are misused?"
Steel
"Yes, we will not infringe on anyone's 2nd amendment rights,
however weapons are to be used strictly for protection
purposes. It should go without saying what will happen to
racers who use their arms with criminal intent."
A
Journalist
"The distance is tremendous... If nobody reaches the finish
line, the sponsors will doubtlessly be furious! All the
trust you've earned will be lost! How would you take
responsibility for that?"
Steel
"...I might have to pay for that... with my life."
Steel
"...That was a joke."
Journalist
Steel
"Failure is- Listen closely! True failure is...! Forgetting
the pioneer spirit! And avoiding challenging hardships!"
Steel
"There is no such thing as failure in this race! There is
only discovery and adventure!"
Steel
"This 'Steel Ball Run' race will be an event the likes of
which this world has never experienced before!"
Young Journalist
"Um! One more question Mr. Steel!"
Young Journalist
"Is the person behind you your wife? I've heard she's 14..."
Steel
"...That has nothing to do with this race."
Aide
"No more questions please! The interview session is over!"
